text.title: aesthetic-minimalist Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for aesthetic-minimalist as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Keeping everything - Fear of removing features
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This leads to cluttered interfaces where important content is lost in noise.

failure-mode.description: Decoration over function - Aesthetics hurting usability
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Visual embellishments distract from content and slow task completion.

failure-mode.description: Hiding too much - Minimalism becomes confusion
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Progressive disclosure taken to extremes hides critical actions from new users, increasing support load.

failure-mode.description: Equal emphasis - Everything looks the same
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without clear hierarchy users cannot distinguish primary content from secondary, prolonging task completion.

failure-mode.description: Ignoring mobile - Desktop clutter on mobile
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mobile screens demand even more minimalism; failing to simplify for mobile creates unusable experiences on constrained devices.

failure-mode.description: "Remove everything" bias: agents applying this heuristic mechanically strip features that power users rely on.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without real usage data, agents apply the heuristic too aggressively to anything that looks decorative.

failure-mode.description: Subjectivity of "noise": what looks decorative to an agent may convey brand trust to users.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Visual branding elements and trust signals are not decorative despite appearing ornamental; their removal damages perception of credibility.

failure-mode.description: Progressive disclosure can hide critical actions from new users, increasing support load.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: What is "advanced" must be validated through user testing; agents guess incorrectly and hide functionality users need immediately.

failure-mode.description: Minimalism applied to forms often removes helpful hints and validation cues, increasing error rates.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Form affordances look like visual noise but serve critical guidance and error prevention functions; removing them increases support load.

failure-mode.description: Cannot substitute for actual task-completion data — a clean UI is not automatically a usable one.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Simplicity audits must be validated against real user behavior metrics; UI simplification without measurement is speculation.
